name: A 1-2-3-4 Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Grease and flour three round pans, 8 or 9 X 1-1/2 inches.
3. In a large bowl, beat all ingredients except Creamy Chocolate Frosting on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the bowl constantly.
4. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
5. Pour batter into pans. If necessary, refrigerate remaining batter while two layers are baking.
6. Bake 8-inch pans for 33 to 36 minutes or until no indentation is left when touched lightly; bake 9-inch pans for 30 to 33 minutes.
7. Cool cakes in pans for 10 minutes, then remove and cool completely on a wire rack.
8. Prepare Creamy Chocolate Frosting by beating all ingredients together until smooth and of spreading consistency. Stir in additional milk, about 1/2 teaspoon at a time, until desired texture is achieved.
9. Fill one layer with approximately 1/3 cup frosting; frost the side and top of the cake with remaining frosting.
12 SERVINGS.
CHOCOLATE FROSTING: Beat all ingredients together on low speed. Stir in additional milk, about 1/2 teaspoon at a time, until smooth and of spreading consistency. This recipe produces enough batter to fill three round cake pans, providing an easy way to remember the proportions for this heavy, fine-textured cake. Before standard measurements were used, bakers often relied on a teacup to ensure uniformity in ingredients.
